examples.title: Algorithm: JDBC-ODBC Database Connection Setup
examples.description: Step-by-step process to establish connection using JDBC-ODBC bridge
examples.code:
ALGORITHM JDBC-ODBC Connection Setup
BEGIN
 1. LOAD JDBC-ODBC Bridge driver class
 2. DEFINE connection URL with DSN
 3. ESTABLISH connection using DriverManager
 4. CREATE Statement object
 5. EXECUTE SQL query
 6. PROCESS ResultSet
 7. CLOSE all resources
END
examples.explanation:
1. Loads the bridge driver class to enable ODBC communication
2. URL format: 'jdbc:odbc:[DataSourceName]'
3. DriverManager handles connection with credentials
4. Statement object created for query execution
5. Execute SQL SELECT/UPDATE
6. Iterate through ResultSet for query results
7. Essential to prevent resource leaks

examples.title: Java: Establishing JDBC-ODBC Connection
examples.description: Complete Java code example for database connection using JDBC-ODBC bridge
examples.explanation:
1. Loads deprecated JDBC-ODBC bridge driver
2. Connection URL uses ODBC Data Source Name (DSN)
3. Creates statement and executes sample query
4. Iterates through result set
5. Proper resource cleanup in finally block (simplified here)
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for setup, O(n) for query processing
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for connection, O(m) for result storage

examples.title: Transaction Handling with JDBC-ODBC
examples.description: Demonstrating transaction management using setAutoCommit and commit
examples.explanation:
1. Disables auto-commit to start transaction
2. Executes multiple updates as atomic operation
3. Commits on success, rolls back on exception
4. Shows basic transaction ACID properties implementation
